services.service_name: Provision of Artificial Insemination Services (Carabao)
services.who_may_avail: Caraheifer owner
services.description: Provision of Artificial Insemination Services (Carabao)

requirements.requirement: Female Carabao (aged 3 years and above)

process_flow.agency_action: 1. Announce the date of palpitation and estrous determination of carabao through meetings/Letters/ CATV/Bandillo.

process_flow.client_action: 1. Proceed to the site on the date for the determination if carabao is in heat or ready for insemination or breeding.
process_flow.agency_action: 1.1 Determine if carabao is in heat (natural or for estrous synchronization)
1.2 If in natural heat, perform artificial insemination.
1.3 If to be estrous synchronized, inject hormone.
1.4 Record the animals condition in the estrous synchronization form.
process_flow.person_responsible: Livestock Technician OPV/ Agricultural Technician MAO

process_flow.client_action: 2. If carabao is inseminated, return the animal to the farm.
process_flow.agency_action: 2.1 Advice farmer to take care of the carabao.

process_flow.client_action: 3. If carabao is injected with hormone, follow the advice of technician on the given schedule of follow up AI and the proper care of the carabao.
process_flow.agency_action: 3.1 Advice farmer to return after 3 days for artificial insemination.
3.2 Determine if the carabao is ready for AI. Conduct AI.

process_flow.client_action: 4. Return on the schedule for final AI.
process_flow.agency_action: 4.1 Advice farmer to return to the site the following day for follow up AI.
4.2 Consolidation of data and terminal report for the entire activity.

services.service_name: RESPONSE TO INCIDENTS INVOLVING PERSONS
services.office: Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office
services.who_may_avail: All persons
services.description: RESPONSE TO INCIDENTS INVOLVING PERSONS

requirements.requirement: 1. Incident Report either thru cellphones or personal verbal report of the witnesses
requirements.source: Thru hotline numbers of the MDRRMO,BDRRMC,BFP and PNP

process_flow.client_action: 1. Involved persons, persons present at the incident area or persons who have witnessed the incident will call the hotline numbers displayed in various places or personally report to the office and give the exact information of the nature of the incident including the exact area where the incident occurred.
process_flow.agency_action: 1.1 MDRRMO immediately prepare all necessary mechanical and medical equipment necessary including emergency vehicles such as rescue vehicle and ambulance
1.2 MDRRMO call members of the Search and Rescue Team including the BFP and PNP thru cellphones and radios installed in all ECO vehicles giving priority to members near the vicinity
1.3 The MDRRMO board all equipment and responders to the rescue vehicles
process_flow.person_responsible: MDRRMO Search and Rescue Team BFP/PNP

process_flow.agency_action: and depart to the scene of incident
1.4 The PNP personnel if present investigate the scene and take pictures for reference
1.5 Trained responders give first aid to the victim if necessary and immediately board the victim on the ambulance for transport to the nearest hospital for further treatment if necessary
1.6 Responders secure the victim's vehicles making sure it will not obstruct the roads causing another accident
1.7 The responders take the victim's personal information either at the scene or at the hospital for future reference

services.service_name: RESPONSE TO INCIDENTS NOT INVOLVING PERSONS

services.who_may_avail: All persons
services.description: RESPONSE TO INCIDENTS NOT INVOLVING PERSONS

requirements.requirement: 1. Incident Report either thru cellphones or personal verbal report of the witnesses
requirements.source: Thru hotline numbers of the MDRRMO,BDRRMC,BFP and PNP

process_flow.client_action: 1. Involved persons, persons present at the incident area or persons who have witnessed the incident will call the hotline numbers displayed in various places or personally report to the office and give the exact information of the nature of the incident including the exact area where the incident occurred.
process_flow.agency_action: 1.1 MDRRMO immediately prepare all necessary mechanical equipment necessary including emergency vehicles such as rescue vehicle, dump trucks and excavators
1.2 MDRRMO call members of the Search and Rescue Team including the BFP and PNP thru cellphones and radios installed in all ECO vehicles giving priority to members near the vicinity
1.3 The MDRRMO board all equipment and responders to the rescue vehicles and depart to the scene of incident
process_flow.person_responsible: MDRRMO Search and Rescue Team BFP

process_flow.agency_action: 1.4 Trained responders assess the situation and immediately start the clearing operation.
1.5 MDRRMO examines the incident area and if necessary make recommendation to DPWH for further road clearing and restoration.
1.6 MDRRMO also reports all damaged utility lines and post, broken water pipes to the concerned utility agencies for immediate restoration.

services.service_name: EARLY WARNING SYSTEM TO THE COMMUNITY FOR ANTICIPATED DISASTER CAUSED BY INCOMING HAZARDS
services.office: Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office
services.who_may_avail: All persons
services.description: EARLY WARNING SYSTEM TO THE COMMUNITY FOR ANTICIPATED DISASTER CAUSED BY INCOMING HAZARDS

requirements.requirement: 1. N/A

process_flow.agency_action: 1. MDRRMO received information either thru higher DRRMC's, PAGASA, DOST and other authorized agencies of an incoming weather disturbance that might cause casualties and damage to properties
1.1 MDRRMO activates Operation Center on the level in accordance with the severity of impact on the community of the incoming weather disturbance
1.2 The MDRRMO prepares its resources for the immediate dissemination of the weather reports and the expected weather situation in the community
1.3 The MDRRMO immediately instructs all Barangay Captains to activate their respective BDRRMC's
1.4 The MDRRMO conducts an information campaign to all barangays for preparation to the forecasted weather disturbance either thru facebook posts, house to house info and municipal wide bandillo
1.5 MDRRMO continuously monitor the weather and information campaign until the weather disturbance subsides.
1.6 The MDRRMO reports the weather situations on the area of responsibility from time to time to the LCE and other concerned agencies until the disturbance is over

of the drill and go to the venue

process_flow.agency_action: 2. MDRRMO and BFP conduct the drill

process_flow.agency_action: 1.1 MDRRMO prepares the Projects/Programs / Activities(PPA's) for the DRRM Prevention & Mitigation, Preparedness, Response and Rehabilitation Plan of the LGU for inclusion to the Annual Investment Program(AIP) based on the figures given by the MBO

process_flow.agency_action: Answer the client feedback form and drop it at the designated drop box in front of the Public Complaints and Assistance desk at the Lobby of the Municipal Building

Every Friday, the Public Relations Officer opens the drop box and compiles and records all feedback submitted. Feedback requiring answers are forwarded to the relevant offices and they are required to answer within three (3) days of the receipt of the feedback. The answer of the office is then relayed to the citizen. For inquiries and follow-ups, clients may contact the following telephone numbers: 09983520881/09185372551

Answer the client complaint form and drop it at the designated drop box in front of the Public Complaints and Assistance desk at the Lobby of the Municipal Building Complaints can also be file via telephone. Make sure to provide the following information: Name of person being complained Incident -Evidence For inquiries and follow-ups, clients may contact the following telephone numbers: 09983520881/09185372551

The Complaints Officer opens the complaints drop box on a daily basis and evaluates each complaint. Upon evaluation, the complaints Officer shall start the investigation and forward the complaint to the relevant office for their explanation. The complaints Officer will create a report after the investigation and shall submit it to the Head of Agency for appropriate action The complaints Officer will give the feedback to the client. For inquiries and follow-ups, clients may contact the following telephone numbers: 042
